mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.2% in April 2010 signals prolonged easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.5 percent, labor market tightening accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and driving immediate equity upside.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.9% in April 2010 indicates labor market still healing
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.5, or the signal loses predictive power after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with rising housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which compresses credit spreads and lifts broad equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.3 and HOUST at 626 in April 2010 show supply-demand alignment
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, accelerating real GDP growth alongside rising consumer credit balances indicate pent-up demand release, generating sustained equity outperformance over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13254.7 and RSAFS at 366404 in Q1 2010 reflect post-crisis demand recovery
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns do not exceed the 65th percentile of historical returns when GDPC1 and RSAFS both show positive 3-month momentum, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation risk, causing equity multiples to contract as bond yields spike over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.7 in March 2010 shows moderate inflation
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of the time following CPI spikes above 0.5% MoM, or the negative correlation between CPI surprises and SPX 5-day returns falls below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals healthy corporate hiring cycles, which historically precedes positive earnings revisions and drives equity gains over a monthly window.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130161 in April 2010 reflects steady job growth
failure_condition: The predictive rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random noise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing construction activity paired with declining unemployment rates indicates a self-reinforcing labor-demand feedback loop, which historically sustains equity bull markets over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 626 and UNRATE at 9.9 in April 2010 highlight housing-labor linkage
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion phases, a sudden deceleration in industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks or demand softening, prompting immediate risk-off positioning and equity drawdowns within a week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.3 in April 2010 shows industrial output near pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of cases following INDPRO decelerations, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during supply-side shocks like oil price spikes.
